Okay, welcome to section 12. This is going to be the last part of this writing workshop. So you just did some really heavy lifting with your product tour, so we're going to try to keep this one nice and tight. But while it's going to be short, it's actually a critical piece that also gets a ton of those skimmer eyeballs on it. So there's really a big payoff in investing energy to get your guarantee right. So since it gets so much attention, we really want to pack as much conversion power as possible. So we have to treat it as so much more than just a simple risk reversal because it is more than that. It's actually a result reminder. You know, people who just arbitrarily slap on a 30 day guarantee and call it a day are missing a big opportunity because it's not just that risk reversal. It's actually a sales piece. It's reminding them of a specific outcome that they can achieve within a specific timeframe by taking specific actions. And when we really zoom in, here's what we're going to be asking this section to coach. So we are coaching affirmation of the big promise. We are also coaching a clear, specific and exciting win for them to work towards. And finally, something I call risk alchemy, which is how to turn risk into resourcefulness and courage to take action. And that's really where the alchemy happens. You can't remove something like risk and not have something fill that vacuum. And we spoke about that give receive dynamic with the sacred cows and empowering beliefs back in an earlier writing workshop. And now we're doing it here. We're removing the risk and inviting resourcefulness and a clear path to take up that space. So how are we going to do all of this? Well I did prepare a general format for us to use. So the first thing you're going to do is you're going to brand your guarantee, give it a name that goes beyond just 30 day guarantee. Next you're going to demonstrate unshakable confidence in your product. Allow your prospect to borrow your confidence. They're going to be a little nervous at this point because your offer was introduced, there's money on the line, you want them to be able to borrow your confidence. You're then going to state the guarantee length. You're going to state the specific actions they'll be taking during that time to produce the outcome. And then you're also going to state the specific emotional and tangible benefits they should see within that time frame. And if that condition isn't met, you're going to state the exact process for getting a refund. Now the contact person should be specific and disarming. You know it's actually a lot safer to ask for a refund from a real person, not just contact. You really do want to give the person a name ideally and this is a little tip. It should not be the actual creator or an author or the author of the course or the program that you're selling. People do find it to be a lot safer and less anxiety provoking to reach out to someone who is a little more neutral in that exchange. And then finally you are going to repeat your USP as a reason for your confidence and I'm going to show you how that looks like in just a sec. So this is the guarantee from our friends at Soul Powered. So we branded it the indestructible guarantee and right off the bat displaying some confidence. We're so passionate about partnering with you to build your indestructible team and want to make it as easy as possible for you to give a confident yes. Enter the indestructible guarantee. Take 30 days specific time frame to go through the first two modules of the indestructible team experience. If after completing the indestructible team guidebook and getting your cues answered on the live coaching calls, very specific things they'll be doing, you still don't feel like you have the insight, clarity or powerful action steps needed to 10x your investment while shaving off at least 10 high stress hours per week. Specific result. Simply email and we remove the name and the email address but specific people with your completed guidebook and we'll hit undo on this whole let's scale your company in a sustainable way experiment. Now another example is back to Greg Kikman. So the get your funnel running and producing profits in 60 days guarantee. So this is branded within the frame of the promise and it goes like this. The promise is simple. Follow the program, upload the ready made funnels we've expertly designed for you and reach out when you need some extra hand holding. So those ready made funnels was part of the USP. If you still don't have your core funnels up and running generating at least 20% more profit within 60 days, specific result giving you the time, confidence and momentum to scale like MoFo. There's your emotional payoff. I'll insist that you email name at email for an immediate refund. And then he continues. Why am I so confident in doing this? One 80% of the work is already done for you. Reminding them of that USP. With this straight out of the box momentum, I'm confident enough that you'll be motivated to do the rest. And the expert funnels strategies you'll be implementing have served as the backbone across multiple six and seven figure core launches for private clients like Pat Lange on the Dumas and Chris Ducker. Final note on guarantees. Nobody wants to be satisfied and nothing makes me cringe more than when I read a copy that says if you aren't satisfied. The satisfaction is vague. What does it even mean and what does satisfaction even look like? In reality, satisfaction is just a generic placeholder catchall for the real experience. No one wants to be satisfied. Satisfied doesn't pay the bills. It doesn't lower your cholesterol and it definitely doesn't make you punch in your credit card details. No one invests hundreds or thousands of dollars for the once in a lifetime opportunity to be satisfied. So instead, describe the experience. Don't make your prospect guess what satisfaction looks like. Be hyper specific. Make it so clear that anyone could instantly get it. You can't coach resourcefulness and ramp up their ability to respond and act if they don't even know the game they're playing or the goal they're pursuing. So it's your job to really set up those yardsticks and say this is what you'll be working towards with my help and if we don't get there, we can hit undo. The quickest way to know you're being specific enough is what I call the sham wow test. The sham wow test essentially goes like this. It applies to all your copy but it really comes to the fruition in your guarantee. So if your guarantee could be placed on a sham wow ad without anyone knowing the difference, you're not trying hard enough. You're not being specific enough and you're making it way too hard for your prospect to tap into what awaits them through working with you. So with all that said, take some time, write your golden guarantee and then meet me in the final writing session in module 6. I'll see you there.
